title: ebenidium lagopus
side_effects: Ebenidium lagopus can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea and dizziness, which may be caused by ingesting too much of the plant's toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's chemicals can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as allergic reactions such as skin irritation and respiratory issues. In severe cases, improper use of ebenidium lagopus can even cause seizures, coma, or death, especially when combined with other medications or substances. It is essential to consult with a medical professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
